prediction_ref.short_label: AMD breaks out AI-accelerator revenue as 10-Q segment footnote by Q3 2026

validation_rows.evidence_summary: AMD's May 5 Q1 2026 print lands the cleanest single-vendor confirmation since the prediction was authored: revenue $10.25B vs $9.85B consensus (+38% YoY), adjusted EPS $1.37 vs $1.27 consensus (+9.6% beat), Data Center segment $5.78B at +57% YoY (consensus $5.56B / +51.5% pace) on EPYC + Instinct GPU shipment ramp, non-GAAP gross margin 55%, GAAP 53%, Q2 2026 guide $11.2B (+/- $300M) vs Street $10.52B (+50.6% YoY at midpoint, +9.1% sequential), AMD shares +4.4% after-hours. CEO Lisa Su explicitly declared Data Center the "primary driver of revenue and earnings growth" and named MI450 Series + Helios customer engagement "strengthening" — but the segment still bundles EPYC server CPUs with Instinct accelerators, leaving buyside models unable to triangulate accelerator-only ASP and unit economics from the bundled line. Pairs with the standing NVIDIA Blackwell deployment scale (~1,000 NVL72 racks/wk, B200 rental ~$6.04/hr down ~6% YoY, B200 architecture lowering cost per million tokens 15x) so the two-vendor AI-accelerator duopoly disclosure shape now has both Q1 lines in the public record on the same day.

With Lisa Su naming Data Center the primary growth driver, the precondition for an accelerator-segment carve-out is now operative on a real print.
bridge.remaining_gap: Need AMD to actually publish accelerator-only revenue, ASP, or unit economics in a 10-Q footnote rather than continuing the bundled segment line.

prediction_ref.short_label: Hyperscaler discloses per-token-margin-gated GPU capex floor by Q3 2026

validation_rows.evidence_summary: AMD's May 5 Q1 print and Q2 guide ($11.2B vs Street $10.52B) plus the ~$700B 2026 Big-Tech AI-capex tape plus NVIDIA's pre-allocation of nearly all B200 production through Q2 2026 to hyperscaler priority (Microsoft, Google, Amazon, Meta) plus the May 4 Anthropic + OpenAI parallel JV launches stack the buyside-pressure case for an explicit per-token-margin disclosure unit. Cost-per-million-tokens has dropped 15x on B200 vs prior generation (~2c/M tokens on GPT-OSS-120B), giving the hyperscalers a quantifiable margin baseline they could publish against, while the AMD Data Center bundle hides Instinct accelerator economics inside a CPU+GPU segment line. The cohort is visibly cracking on the disclosure side but no Big-3 hyperscaler has yet committed to a Bedrock / Azure / Vertex per-token-margin floor as a capex gate.

prediction_ref.short_label: Microsoft Agent 365 GA forces Big-3 to ship default MCP policy by Q4 2026

validation_rows.evidence_summary: Cisco's May 4 ~$400M intent to acquire Astrix Security converts non-human-identity (NHI) governance into an explicit M&A category alongside Microsoft Agent 365's May 1 GA at $15/user/mo M365 E7 SKU; planned integration into Cisco Identity Intelligence + Cisco Secure Access + Duo IAM covers discovery + lifecycle management for agentic access, threat detection on out-of-scope agent behavior, and centralized secrets management. Cisco's customer survey shows 85% experimenting with AI agents but only 5% in production with AI agents + non-human identities outnumbering human users ~100:1, while industry baseline of ~7,000 internet-exposed MCP servers (roughly half with no authorization controls) sets the structural urgency. Three-vendor convergence (Cisco + Microsoft + Astrix) on the agent-identity-as-first-pillar Zero Trust thesis is now visible inside one week. AWS Bedrock + Google Vertex have not yet published parallel tenant-level policy-enforcement schemas.

prediction_ref.short_label: Cross-cloud agent-identity + MCP registry converges into draft RFC by Q3 2026

validation_rows.evidence_summary: Cisco's May 4 $400M Astrix Security deal makes non-human-identity discovery + governance an M&A-tier primitive on the same week as Microsoft Agent 365 GA and the Cloud Security Alliance's May 1 framing of identity as Zero Trust's first pillar in the agentic-AI era; Astrix capability set covers API keys, service accounts, OAuth tokens, machine-to-machine credentials, plus threat detection on out-of-scope agent behavior. prediction_ref.short_label: CISA + NIST publish inference-server + agentic-tool CVE sub-catalog by Q4 2026

validation_rows.evidence_summary: SGLang CVE-2026-5760 (CVSS 9.8 RCE via /v1/rerank with malicious GGUF, jinja2.Environment without sandboxing as root cause) lands its Apr 24 fix commit confirmed in the NVD detail today, swapping in ImmutableSandboxedEnvironment per CERT/CC mitigation guidance — disclosure-to-fix window finally closes after running ~15 days as the longest-2026-unpatched LLM-tooling CVE, with supply-chain risk vector (malicious GGUF distributed via model repositories) still standing. Pairs with the May 2026 industry baseline of ~7,000 internet-exposed MCP servers (roughly half with no authorization controls) and the 2026 AI-CVE forecast of 2,800-3,600 disclosures (31-69% YoY increase from 2025) with MCP-server + agentic-AI categories the fastest-growing, validating that an inference-server + agentic-tool CVE sub-corpus is operationally distinguishable. No CISA or NIST issuance lands today.

prediction_ref.short_label: KV-cache-compression dtype ships as inference-stack deployment knob by Q4 2026

validation_rows.evidence_summary: Cloudflare's Rust-based Infire LLM inference engine ships across the global edge network with prefill/decode disaggregation (separating input-processing + output-generation onto different optimized systems), benchmarking 7% faster than vLLM 0.10.0 on unloaded H100 NVL machines and significantly better under real load — adds a third inference-engine candidate alongside vLLM and SGLang and reframes the production-serving Pareto split into a three-way (SGLang for shared-prefix / agentic, vLLM for batch / multi-hardware, Infire for edge-distributed serving with prefill/decode separation). Standing SGLang vs vLLM 29% throughput gap on H100 (RadixAttention prefix-cache the structural advantage for shared-context workloads) carries forward; no fresh kv-cache-dtype CLI flag ships today.
